title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
summary: NVIDIA's approach reduces diffusion model denoising steps from fifty to as few as one by combining dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching to skip recomputation, and student-teacher distillation. The technique achieves ten to two hundred times speedup enabling near real-time video generation on Blackwell B200, with gains described as additive so methods can be applied independently or stacked.
writeup: Ziv Ilan of NVIDIA presents methods for cutting diffusion model denoising from fifty steps to as few as one, combining dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching that skips recomputation, and student-teacher distillation. The result is a ten to two hundred times speedup that enables near real-time video generation on Blackwell B200. The key point: the gains are additive, so each technique can be applied alone or stacked together.
